subject: [DOC] Add a link to index
commit_message:
[DOC] Add a link to index

New documentation generation system relies on disjoint documentations.
This patch adds an label for OOM main index with name being consistent
accross all ONAP components.

Issue-ID: CIMAN-376
Change-Id: I1c13b2c478857c45a30ec5e5fb4881934e6f70f3
Signed-off-by: lf-jobbuilder <releng+lf-jobbuilder@linuxfoundation.org>
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I’m pretty sure there’s a good reason but commit message (nor jira ticket) explains the purpose. 
So, add an explanation in the commit message in why we do this commit (like for every commit we do)

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> I’m pretty sure there’s a good reason but commit message (nor jira ticket) explains the purpose. 
> So, add an explanation in the commit message in why we do this commit (like for every commit we do)

commits were made by and automated script, there are 100 or so, I can't make a better explanation,
Jessica will fill you in, but feel free to merge.

Patch Set 2:

@Sylvain, I have added more information under the jira ticket. 
Basically this is related to the email I sent earlier where we are looking forward to standardize the docs across projects to be able to add more automation to it. At first, it will require mass changes like this one, but this is a step needed in order for us to have more consistency across everyone. You can find more information here:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Docs+migration+tracker?src=contextnavpagetreemode

Once we are consistent using matching labels, we can start applying automated scripts to fix all broken linkages with minimal work.

Again, I do understand the need for the change, I just want a better commit message as that’s the only thing that will last with the code. 
So please make a better commit message for this one and other and I’ll merge. 

Too bad that you didn’t do it during the automated phase as good commit message are important for every projects.
